chapters.timespan: Single day, from a morning frontier patrol to a late-day interrogation threshold at the southern border post.
keyEvents: Sylvanas finishes a troll sweep on the southeastern frontier while dodging a Convocation session.
A Farstrider runner reports that a human mage crossed to the second ward ring before dawn and is in custody at Cyndia's post.
Sylvanas rides south, confirms the prisoner is Jaina Proudmoore, and opens the interrogation.

chapters.timespan: The same evening into deepening dusk, from interrogation inside the ward cell to the mounted approach on Millhaven.
keyEvents: Jaina convinces Sylvanas to test the warning in the field and is released under armed escort.
The party rides south out of Thalassian Pass, confirming the contamination is broader than the wards allowed them to feel.
A wildlife attack proves the corruption is active in living flesh before death.
The same mounted group reaches visual approach on a silent Millhaven.

chapters.timespan: The same evening into nightfall, from the party's entry into Millhaven through the survivor rescue, warning dispatch, and renewed southbound ride.
keyEvents: Sylvanas orders a full sweep of Millhaven and confirms the village is intact, contaminated, and emptied of bodies.
Miri is recovered alive from a cellar, and her testimony plus Jaina's read and Cyndia's spoor work confirm that the dead marched north toward Quel'Thalas.
Sylvanas dispatches Neral and the unnamed escort ranger north with the warning and survivor, then rides south with Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel toward Corin's Crossing.

chapters.timespan: A single day, two days after chapter3: morning arrival in a still-living village through nightfall decision to ride for Andorhal at first light.
keyEvents: Sylvanas wins access to a living but infected village by invoking Millhaven and splitting the investigation between sickroom and granary records.
Jaina proves the plague is entering through tainted grain and buys time with stabilization wards.
The granary reveals concentrated plague agent and a live watcher, Davan, who dies by poison before interrogation.
The surviving party leaves written instructions behind and fixes Andorhal as the next pursuit target.

chapters.timespan: A single long investigative day into night, from the first depot south of Millhaven through the route choice at the waystation before dawn departure.
keyEvents: Sylvanas and Jaina turn manifests, route marks, and contamination notes into a regional map of the grain road.
Crossbar and Millbrook Spur prove the enemy is cleaning depots ahead of the investigation and killing logistics witnesses.
The Barov name appears on surviving route evidence without yet becoming proven guilt.
The party chooses the southwest feeder road near Strahnbrad as the approach into Andorhal's network.

chapters.timespan: A single travel day from the waystation through Strahnbrad's military zone to first sight of Andorhal at the edge of evening approach.
keyEvents: Jaina locks the plague model into grain, body, death, and organized support labor while the party rides deeper into the basin roads.
At Strahnbrad, Jaina briefs Arthas, who understands the warning and still refuses to divert force from the visible war.
A second grain-road contact repeats Safe roads. What's left of them., confirming the farewell as recurring network language.
The party reaches a still-working Andorhal and turns fully toward records, towers, and signatories inside the city.

chapters.timespan: A single day and night inside Andorhal, from covert gate entry through the planning of a same-night capture operation.
keyEvents: The party enters Andorhal under cover and confirms the city is a functioning logistical hub rather than a dead endpoint.
Corin and Havel's separate warnings lock together when Jaina proves packaging-stage contamination and Sylvanas maps the hidden local cult cell.
The chapter closes on a split same-night plan: Jaina reads the treatment building while Sylvanas moves to take a live district leader.
